pattern: Reducing every string to one small integer score lets a sorted-plus-binary-search pass answer each query in logarithmic time.
hints: Each string collapses to a single number: how many times its smallest letter appears. Compute that score for everything first.
For a query score q you need the count of word scores strictly greater than q; sorting the word scores makes that a search instead of a scan.
Sort the word scores, then for each query binary-search the first score above q; the count above is the total minus that position.

edge_inputs.description: multiple queries over graded word scores
ties: equal scores are not counted as greater
all words score below every query
smallest char buried among larger letters

stress.description: many short random lowercase strings for both lists
stress.note: narrow alphabet creates frequent score ties
